quick_facts.label: Official programme name
quick_facts.value: At the National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT), the degree is offered as Bachelor of Fashion Technology (Apparel Production), or B.F.Tech.

quick_facts.label: Duration
quick_facts.value: NIFT describes B.F.Tech (Apparel Production) as a four-year undergraduate programme.

quick_facts.label: Core eligibility
quick_facts.value: For regular entry, applicants need a recognised 10+2/equivalent qualification with Physics and Mathematics; the 2026 UG age limit was under 24 years on 1 August 2026, with a five-year relaxation for SC/ST/PwD candidates.

quick_facts.label: Entrance route
quick_facts.value: For NIFT Admissions 2026, B.F.Tech selection was based on the computer-based General Ability Test (GAT); there was no CAT, Situation Test, or interview for B.F.Tech regular admission.

quick_facts.label: Career orientation
quick_facts.value: The programme is oriented to apparel-manufacturing and techno-managerial work, including production planning, merchandising, quality control, supply chain, industrial engineering, product development, and entrepreneurship.

sections.heading: Eligibility for regular B.F.Tech entry
paragraphs: For NIFT’s 2026 regular B.F.Tech (Apparel Production) admission, candidates needed to have passed Class 12/10+2 or an accepted equivalent with both Physics and Mathematics. Accepted routes included a recognised central/state board, the National Open School senior-secondary examination with at least five subjects, an AIU-recognised equivalent, or GCE A-level/International Baccalaureate qualifications with Physics and Mathematics. Candidates taking their qualifying board examination in the admission year could apply provisionally, subject to meeting the requirements at seat allocation/admission.

For the 2026 admission cycle, the upper-age condition for UG programmes was that the candidate be under 24 on 1 August 2026; SC, ST and PwD candidates received a five-year relaxation. Eligibility policies can change by admission year, so applicants should use the current NIFT prospectus rather than relying on the 2026 conditions.
bullets: Physics and Mathematics are required for the regular B.F.Tech route; a general Class 12 qualification without those subjects does not meet this stated requirement.

NIFT also publishes a separate lateral-entry route. Its stated B.F.Tech lateral-entry criteria include a relevant 3- or 4-year diploma after Class 10, or qualifying Class 12 with Physics and Mathematics plus completion of NIFT’s two-year UG diploma programme.

sections.heading: Programme duration and academic character
paragraphs: B.F.Tech (Apparel Production) at NIFT is a four-year undergraduate programme. It is technology-oriented and multidisciplinary, built around the apparel business and the operational realities of apparel manufacturing rather than being solely a fashion-design degree.
The programme includes a final-year graduation project intended to address real-world problems in apparel design and development, manufacturing, or related areas.
bullets: Award: Bachelor of Fashion Technology (Apparel Production).

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: NIFT describes the B.F.Tech curriculum as combining apparel-production technology with managerial and business foundations. Its 2026 prospectus identifies mandatory study in apparel production technology, production planning, pattern making, fashion marketing, sourcing and costing; NIFT also states that specialised machinery and IT laboratories support hands-on learning.

The published programme description additionally identifies fabrics and trims, apparel machinery, quality management, information technology, artificial intelligence, work study, merchandising, production planning and quality control as important areas. Students may have opportunities for interdisciplinary minors across fields such as Fashion Management Studies, Fashion Design, Fashion Communication and Accessory Design, subject to NIFT’s academic offerings and rules.
bullets: Manufacturing and operations: apparel-production technology, machinery, production planning and control.
Commercial and supply functions: sourcing, costing, merchandising and fashion marketing.
Quality and process improvement: quality management/control, work study and industrial/operational practices.
Applied learning: laboratories and a final-year problem-oriented graduation project.

sections.heading: Admission through the NIFT Entrance Examination
paragraphs: For the 2026 cycle, NIFT used the General Ability Test (GAT) as the sole regular-entry test for B.F.Tech. The official FAQ states that GAT is computer based and assesses quantitative ability, analytical ability, logical reasoning, English comprehension, general knowledge and current affairs; final B.F.Tech merit was based 100% on the GAT score.

This differs from B.Des admission: B.F.Tech candidates do not take the Creative Ability Test (CAT), Situation Test or interview for the regular B.F.Tech route. A candidate with Mathematics who also applied to B.Des could take CAT together with GAT and receive separate merit ranks for B.Des and B.F.Tech.

The 2026 NIFTEE application window ran from 8 December 2025 to 6 January 2026, with a late-fee window through 10 January 2026; the entrance examination was scheduled for 8 February 2026. These are past, cycle-specific dates and should not be used as a forecast for the next intake.
bullets: Apply through NIFT/NTA’s admissions process for the applicable year.
Take GAT for B.F.Tech regular admission.
Use the official prospectus, admissions page and counselling notices for current dates, seat matrix, category rules and document requirements.

sections.heading: Fees: published 2026 context
paragraphs: Fees are institution-, category- and year-specific. In NIFT’s 2026 prospectus, the published non-NRI regular fee schedule lists tuition of ₹150,000 per semester for 2026–27, alongside annual library, mediclaim/student-development and examination charges. The prospectus shows grand totals of ₹191,700 for the applicable first listed semester and ₹150,000 for the following semester, including the relevant one-time charges in the first amount.

The same published schedule states that NIFT may revise fees for any academic year. These figures should therefore be treated as dated 2026 admission context, not as a guaranteed total programme cost; housing, meals, travel, materials and other personal expenses are separate planning considerations unless specifically included in a current campus notice.
bullets: Published first listed semester grand total: ₹191,700; following semester grand total: ₹150,000.
Confirm the current prospectus and campus-specific notices before paying or budgeting.

sections.heading: Career and further-study options
paragraphs: NIFT identifies apparel-manufacturing career paths including sampling-room coordinator, fit analyst, product-development manager, shop-floor manager, production planner/controller, merchandiser, supply-chain manager, retail planner, quality controller/quality-assurance professional, industrial engineer/manager, consultant and entrepreneur. These describe possible role directions rather than guaranteed placement outcomes.

For further study at NIFT, a B.F.Tech is an eligible qualification for M.F.Tech. NIFT’s 2026 rules also state that an undergraduate degree in any discipline can meet the academic eligibility for M.F.M, subject to the programme’s entrance and admission process. Graduates interested in building a fashion label or consultancy can also use the production, sourcing, costing, quality and merchandising capabilities developed in the programme; NIFT notes that some B.F.Tech graduates establish clothing brands, technical-textile businesses, consultancy firms and apparel-technology ventures.
bullets: Employment directions: apparel production, quality assurance, sourcing/merchandising, product development, operations and supply chain.
Entrepreneurship direction: fashion label, manufacturing-linked venture, consultancy or apparel-technology business.
Further study direction: M.F.Tech; M.F.M is also academically open to holders of an undergraduate degree, subject to current NIFT requirements.
